# Taylor (NotGreg) + Hannah — February 19, 2026

**Type:** Relationship building / Business exchange
**Participants:** Dax, Taylor, Hannah (Taylor's sister, 19, his 50% business partner)
**Taylor's Discord:** NotGreg

---

## Overview

Taylor joined with his sister Hannah partway through the call. The conversation covered DD's business in more depth, Taylor offered a consulting perspective on DD's retention problem, and both sides explored what a mutual exchange of value could look like. The PA connection was discovered — Taylor is from Collegeville/Westchester, same area as Dax's family.

---

## Taylor's Take on DD

Taylor's primary observation: DD is giving too much away for free. With ~8,000 members and ~50 paying, the gap signals that free content eliminates urgency to pay. His recommendation: paywall more, communicate the "hands-on instructor" value more explicitly, and charge for the confidence/guidance Dax provides — not just the tools.

He also noted DD's interaction style (Dax engaging members, teaching them, answering questions) is itself worth charging for — citing Gary Vee's point that people will literally pay to walk alongside someone they trust.

---

## DD Business Context Shared

- Raising price to $50/month imminently (from $15/month, unchanged for 3 years)
- ~80% of traffic organic from Fliff Discord SEO + Discord listing sites (Discadia, Discord.me, Hive Index)
- ~65% accuracy on sent alerts in 2025, targeting 70% this season
- Building EV filters to cut 2,000 daily alerts down to 5–10/sport — nearly complete
- Staff trimmed from 10–12 to 6; commission-only model, independent contractors (no W9 yet, under threshold)
- Developer Zach: recently transitioned from factory job to software role at his employer; will have less time for DD but more relevant work experience
- Dax is learning Claude/coding to handle more of the codebase himself

---

## Dax's Fintech Internship

The company Dax interns at is essentially a "savings account for gamblers" — users deposit money, the company gives sports picks, and returns are based on whether picks win (interest if yes, 0% if no). Still working on its value proposition. Taylor was interested and wants to connect his development partners (Sumer Labs, based in Collegeville PA) with the company.

---

## PA Connection

Taylor worked at the Collegeville Best Buy 2014–15, parties in the Westchester area, and plans to sublet a room in Collegeville this year for client work. Dax grew up in Collegeville — his parents still live there. Both acknowledged the coincidence.

---

## What Taylor Offered

- **Free business/banking counsel** — ongoing, in exchange for Discord building help
- **Digital bank** (Wells Fargo-affiliated, no brick+mortar): 1% cash back debit, instant payroll deposits, zero-interest loans against upcoming direct deposits; will send links for Dax to research independently
- **Organic member growth** — sending his degenerate gambler friends to DD
- **Staff referrals** — help sourcing knowledgeable bettor candidates
- **Fintech intro** — wants to connect Sumer Labs with Dax's internship company
- **Beta testing** — offered himself and his serious-bettor friends as beta testers

---

## What Dax Offered

- Help Taylor structure and build his Discord server (screen share walkthrough at leisure)
- Intro to the fintech internship company if Taylor wants to be a beta tester/interviewee

---

## Follow-Up Plan

- Stay in touch via Discord DMs or text; Taylor prefers text, has Discord notifications off
- Taylor to send DD his business banking links for Dax to review
- Dax will send Taylor a Google Calendar link for the fintech internship interview
- Discord walkthrough at Dax's leisure — no hard timeline
